title: Gifted and Talented Students (Local)
description: This policy governs how the school district identifies, serves, and when necessary, exits students from its Gifted and Talented (GT) program, including the processes for appealing those decisions and how the program is funded. It applies to students currently enrolled in or being considered for GT services, as well as their parents and guardians. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines their rights to appeal if their child is removed from the GT program and helps them understand how the district manages and resources these specialized educational services.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy EHBB (Local) were adopted unanimously at the November 18, 2025 Regular Board Meeting as part of a group adoption (Motion #7207). No specific language changes are described in the meeting minutes.
timeline_local.notes: Adopted as part of a group motion (#7207) along with BBE, CAA, CDA, CQB, EHB, and GKB. Vote was 7-0. The specific redline changes are not described in these minutes pages.

timeline_local.summary: This is a cover/agenda slide for a Second Reading of nine Local board policies for Conroe ISD. The policies span topics including board governance, fiscal management, revenues, cybersecurity, employee conduct, curriculum, gifted & talented programs, and campus conduct/advertising. No redline text or specific language changes are visible on this page.
timeline_local.discussion: policy and a superintendent Facebook post
timeline_local.notes: This is a table of contents / agenda slide (page 128) listing policies under 'Second Reading.' It does not contain redline text or specific policy language. Detailed changes would appear in subsequent pages of the packet for each individual policy.

timeline_local.summary: This page contains redline changes to the gifted and talented program policy covering Exit Provisions, Appeals, and Funding sections. Key changes include: (1) updating terminology from 'selection' to 'placement' committee; (2) revising exit standard language from 'best interest of the student to exit' to 'not meeting the student's educational needs'; (3) updating appeals language from 'selection for or exit from' to 'services in'; (4) overhauling the Funding section to require the Superintendent or designee to develop administrative procedures aligned with TEA financial compliance guidance, and adding a requirement that 100% of state G/T funds be spent on G/T services or program enhancement.
changes_added: is not meeting the student's educational needs
services in the gifted and talented program (in Appeals section)

Superintendent or designee shall develop administrative procedures to account for the expenditure of the gifted and talented allotment in alignment with the Texas Education Agency's financial compliance guidance.
One hundred percent of state funds allocated for gifted and talented education shall be spent on providing gifted and talented services or enhancing the District's gifted and talented program.
placement (added before 'selection' in 'placement selection committee' in the Selection section)
placement (added before 'selection' in 'placement selection committee' in the Interdistrict section)
identification (replacing 'assessment' in the phrase 'provides an overview of the identification procedures and services for the program')
changes_removed: it is in the best interest of the student to exit the program
selection for or exit from (in Appeals section)
program shall address effective use of funds for programs and services consistent with the standards in the state plan for gifted and talented students.
selection (struck through as part of 'placement selection committee' in the Selection section — replacing standalone 'selection committee')
selection (struck through as part of 'placement selection committee' in the Interdistrict section)
assessment (in the phrase 'provides an overview of the assessment procedures and services for the program')
timeline_local.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline_local.notes: This is page 3 of the policy document, page 264 of the board packet, Policy Update Page Number 28. The Furloughs, Program Evaluation, and Community Awareness sections appear unchanged (shown in black text). The redline changes are shown in red text. The specific policy code is not visible on this page; EHBB is inferred from context.

description_local: This policy sets the rules for how a school district identifies, serves, and supports students in its gifted and talented program, including how students can enter or exit the program and how families can appeal placement decisions. It applies to students who are nominated or selected for gifted and talented services, as well as their parents and the staff who work with them. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines their rights to challenge decisions about their child's participation in the program and explains how the district funds and maintains these specialized services.
